params._note: start_x/start_y centre the ROI on the beam's parked position, sensor ~(y 520, x 952) with the DAC biases below (coarse ch2=0.8 V, ch3=2.394 V). If the coarse biases change, re-derive these with a probe frame (0.7 V on ch2 moved the beam to y 507/x 947). 32x32 was tried 2026-07-10 and reverted: frame rate only rises 2400->~3060 Hz, delay drops just ~0.25 ms (camera fixed latency + DAC ZOH dominate), performance is identical, and the stray reflection ends up only 11 px from the beam. Remember CoM normalization: at 32x32 loop gain doubles, so i/iy and line gains must be halved (i=300/iy=700 was stable there).

pipeline._comment: Send raw frame to watch_steering.jl on port 64730
pipeline._note: decimation must MATCH the 64731 sink below, so the frame and the CoM cross drawn together come from the same iteration. 64 gives ~36 Hz at a 2310 Hz loop -- about what a GR heatmap can redraw.

pipeline._comment: Track a 15x15 window centred on the previous CoM, so the stray reflection never enters the sum.
pipeline._note: Output stays normalized across the whole 64x64 image, NOT the window: the setpoint is still frame centre and the PID gains below are unchanged. With the beam-centred ROI the stray reflection sits INSIDE the frame at ~(10, 28) and carries flux comparable to the beam, so the old whole-frame acquisition phase can settle between the two spots -- init the window on the beam (frame centre) explicitly instead. The reflection is 22 px from the beam, well outside the 15 px window.

pipeline._comment: Send [y, x] CoM vector to watch_steering.jl on port 64731
pipeline._note: keep decimation equal to the 64730 sink above

pipeline._comment: Send [y, x] CoM vector to watch_error_ts.jl on port 64732
pipeline._note: NOT decimated: this is a time series, and dropping samples without an anti-alias filter folds high-frequency error back down into the band you're trying to read. The packets are ~48 bytes, so full rate is ~110 kB/s -- cheap.

adaptive predictor (see doc/devices/kalman_filter.md): replaces the error with its own prediction ~2.1 ms (5 samples) ahead, so the pid acts on where the beam will be when the command lands. A/B-validated 2026-07-10: x steady-state RMS 0.91 -> 0.61 px alone, 0.59 with the pid line oscillators (they coexist fine); the 20-25 Hz lines drop ~5x. gainy is 0 because prediction on y PUMPED its 30.8 Hz line 3x -- y's ~62 Hz crossover loop reacts badly to the extra phase dynamics. Trains continuously; hands over along a 10 s ramp after start_delay.

start_delay must be >= the piplate start_delay below. Holding the fine DAC channels without also holding the pid is useless: the integrator keeps winding on the large startup error behind the parked output, and the fine channel is at its rail the instant the hold expires. Base params are the x axis (element 1), *y the y axis (element 0). i sets the crossover: f_c = i*|P|/2pi with |P|x=0.43, |P|y=0.30.

params._lines: narrowband below said 59.63 before internal-model rejection: one adaptive quadrature oscillator per measured disturbance line. Frequencies from 60 s high-res PSDs 2026-07-10; phases = measured bode plant phase + sensitivity phase at i=600/iy=1300 (empirically trimmed for 59.63). If i/iy change, the phases move -- re-derive or re-trim. Gain sets notch width ~g*|P*S|/pi Hz: 60 is fine below crossover, but 20 max near/above it (59.63) or the notch's own waterbed pumps 62-70 Hz.

params._note1: pid is NOT in coarse mode, so its output is plain [y, x]: ch0 (y fine, +2 V/unit) takes index 0, ch1 (x fine, -2 V/unit) index 1 -- verified by step test 2026-07-10 (ch0 -> CoM y, ch1 -> CoM x, positive i = negative feedback on both). Coarse scales are 0 (parked at bias) per the push-test rule: they had been left live at +-2 with unverified sign, which risks positive feedback; with the beam-centred ROI the fine stages have full authority and the coarse stages are not needed. The coarse biases (0.8, 2.394 V) are the ones the ROI position was derived under.
params._note2: baud 460800 is about pacing, not the wire: the port is USB CDC (baud is cosmetic) but the bridge throttles itself to ~440 cmds/s at the default 115200 -- at a 2310 Hz loop that is a ~2.3 ms command ZOH stacked on the 1.4 ms path delay. Measured 2026-07-10: the bridge drains ~1800 cmds/s; pacing at 460800 (~1770/s) keeps latency flat, while 921600 overruns the drain rate and bufferbloats ~10 ms into a full FIFO. Do not raise past 460800.

params._note: log the filtered command stream; move this before the biquad to capture the raw error for an FFT
